title: Beurre Noisette Schnitzel
description: A classic German dish with a rich, buttery twist. Schnitzel is a breaded and fried cutlet of meat, typically served with a side of lemon wedges and fries.
instructions: 1. Season the pork cutlets with salt and pepper.
2. In a shallow dish, mix together the flour and a pinch of salt and pepper.
3. Dip each pork cutlet in the flour mixture, coating both sides evenly.
4. Dip the floured cutlet in the beaten eggs, making sure to coat completely.
5. Roll the egg-coated cutlet in the breadcrumbs, pressing gently to adhere.
6. Heat the beurre noisette in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
7. Add the breaded pork cutlets to the skillet and cook for 2-3 minutes on each side, or until golden brown and cooked through.
8. Remove the cutlets from the skillet and place on a paper towel-lined plate to drain excess oil.
9. Serve immediately, garnished with lemon wedges and fries on the side.
